Noha Zaki is a Senior Network Engineer with contact information including email (noha.zaki@email.com), phone number (+20 101 234 5678), and links to her GitHub (github.com/noha-zaki) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/noha-zaki) profiles, as well as a personal website (nohazaki.com).

Noha Zaki obtained a Master of Science in Distance Education from AISECT University Gwalior, India between 2012 and 2014, and a Bachelor of Commerce from Jiwaji University Gwalior, India between 2009 and 2012.

From January 2021 to the present, Noha Zaki worked as a Sr. Network Engineer at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., where she led router, switch, and server configuration for Railwire/Railtel Corporation of India Ltd. in Mumbai, managed earthing and trenching, and prepared Wi-Fi environments at railway stations.

From January 2017 to May 2019, the individual executed router, switch, and server configurations with group policies for The Scindia School in Gwalior, implementing and maintaining backup schedules, upgrading Cisco router configurations, and administering Windows and Linux servers.

From January 2015 to December 2016, as a Network Support Engineer, the individual implemented and configured networks for M/s Teva API India Pvt Ltd in Bhind, maintained backups, upgraded Cisco router configurations, administered Windows and Linux servers, and troubleshooted LAN infrastructure.
From September 2013 to December 2014, as a System Engineer, the individual managed servers, backups, and helpdesk support, hosted client meetings, and prepared reports for Nava Bharat Press (P) Ltd.
The individual managed and troubleshooted systems to efficiently resolve issues and also troubleshooted hardware problems to minimize downtime.
The individual holds a Tally ERP9 Certification in both Manual and Computerized Accounting, obtained from Tally Academy in 2013.
The individual possesses skills in various Operating Systems including Windows, Linux, Ubuntu, and RHEL 6.

The individual is proficient in Networking Technologies such as Cisco Routing & Switching, InterVLAN Routing, RIPv2, RIPng, OSPF, EIGRP, Static Routing, ACL, VTP, VLAN, EtherChannel, HSRP, STP, IPv6, LAN Troubleshooting, Structured Network Cabling, Cisco Firewall, and Fortinet Firewall.

The individual is familiar with Networking Devices including Cisco Routers (1800s, 1900s, 2600s, 2900s, 3600s, 3800s, 7200s), Cisco Switches (Catalyst 2900s, 3700s, 4850s), HP ProLiant Servers, Dell Servers, Lenovo Servers, Fortinet Firewall, and Modem.

At Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., Noha Zaki implemented and maintained backup schedules, upgraded Cisco router configurations, administered Windows Servers 2008 R2, 2012 R2, and 2016, troubleshooted LAN & WAN issues, configured Cisco Routers and Switches, conducted health checks on servers, and managed Windows 7 & 10 environments.

From June 2019 to December 2020, Noha Zaki worked as an IT Executive at Asha MMPC in Bali, Pali, Rajasthan, where she managed routers, switches, and servers, configured DPMCU, Milknet Server, EMS Server, and QMS Server, and implemented backup schedules.
The individual has expertise in Server Technologies such as Active Directory (AD, RODC), FTP, Print Server, SCCM, WDS, DHCP, Group Policies, DNS Server, and RIS Server.
The individual is skilled in Backup Technologies including Windows Server 2016 backup tools and Symantec Backup EXEC 12D.
The individual is proficient in Virtualization technologies such as VMware ESXi, VMware Workstation, Oracle VirtualBox, and GNS3 Network Simulator.

At Asha MMPC, Noha Zaki upgraded Cisco router configurations, administered Windows Servers 2008 R2, 2012 R2, and 2016, troubleshooted LAN & WAN issues, configured networking devices, performed health checks on servers, and managed Windows 7 & 10 environments.

Noha Zaki is a seasoned Senior Network Engineer, a meticulous architect of connectivity with a proven track record across diverse environments. Educated with a Master of Science in Distance Education from AISECT University Gwalior and a Bachelor of Commerce from Jiwaji University, she blends a strong technical foundation with a broad understanding of operational needs. 

Currently at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., Noha leads the configuration of routers, switches, and servers, notably spearheading critical infrastructure projects for Railwire/Railtel Corporation of India Ltd., including Wi-Fi deployment in bustling railway stations and meticulous management of earthing and trenching. Her expertise encompasses the full lifecycle of network administration – from implementing and maintaining robust backup schedules and upgrading Cisco configurations, to troubleshooting complex LAN & WAN issues and conducting thorough server health checks. She’s fluent in Windows Server environments (2008 R2, 2012 R2, and 2016) and adept at managing Windows 7 & 10 systems.

Prior to Cloudatix, Noha honed her skills as an IT Executive at Asha MMPC, where she managed core networking infrastructure, configured specialized servers like DPMCU and Milknet, and maintained the smooth operation of critical systems.  

Driven and resourceful, Noha maintains an active online presence through her GitHub (github.com/noha-zaki) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/noha-zaki) profiles, and a personal website (nohazaki.com). She can be reached at noha.zaki@email.com or +20 101 234 5678.

Windows Server 2008 R2 appears as a foundational, though increasingly legacy, element within a network administration skillset. It’s a workhorse, reliably serving core network functions – acting as a central authority for Active Directory, facilitating file transfer via FTP, managing DNS resolution, automating IP address allocation through DHCP, and supporting network booting with TFTP. This server isn’t a flashy newcomer; it’s a seasoned veteran, consistently maintained and relied upon across diverse environments. 

Noha Zaki demonstrates a particular proficiency with this platform, repeatedly taking responsibility for its administration at multiple organizations – Asha MMPC and Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd. – alongside newer server iterations like 2012 R2 and 2016. This suggests a comfort level with its intricacies and a capacity to keep it operational alongside more modern systems. Beyond simply keeping the lights on, Zaki’s role extends to proactive server health checks, indicating a commitment to preventative maintenance and long-term stability. While newer technologies emerge, Windows Server 2008 R2, in Zaki’s hands, remains a vital component of a functioning network infrastructure.

A quiet guardian of data, Backups represent a consistent thread throughout this engineer’s early career. Not flashy, but fundamentally vital, they were a core responsibility at both Nava Bharat Press (P) Ltd and Teva API India Pvt Ltd. At Nava Bharat, Backups weren’t simply a task, but a managed service, integral to keeping the press running and client confidence high. Later, at Teva API, Backups formed part of a broader systems administration role, a foundational element alongside network configuration and server maintenance. This individual relied on the robust capabilities of Symantec Backup Exec 12D to ensure data preservation, demonstrating a practical, hands-on approach to safeguarding critical information. They weren’t just *doing* backups; they were entrusted with the continuity of operations for two distinct organizations, a silent protector against data loss and downtime. Their experience suggests a methodical, detail-oriented nature – someone who understands the unseen importance of preventative measures and reliable recovery.

The Cisco Router stands as a steadfast, if largely unseen, architect of digital pathways. A workhorse of network infrastructure, it’s a device repeatedly called upon to ensure seamless communication, a role consistently entrusted to Noha Zaki. Across diverse environments – the academic grounds of The Scindia School, the pharmaceutical facilities of Teva API India, and the operational cores of Cloudatix Network and Asha MMPC – this router has been a central point of configuration and maintenance. 

It isn’t a static entity, however. Its core functionality is frequently *enhanced* through upgraded configuration files, a process Noha Zaki has repeatedly undertaken, breathing new life and capability into the device. It’s a pragmatic tool, equally comfortable directing traffic within a local area network (LAN) as it is navigating the wider world of wide area networks (WAN), troubleshooting connectivity issues as they arise. 

Beyond simply routing data, it’s a component deeply interwoven with server health, functioning alongside Windows and Linux servers – from the older 2008 R2 iterations to the more modern 2016 versions – and subject to regular health checks. It’s a silent partner in backup schedules, ensuring data resilience, and a key element in maintaining stable Windows 7, 8, and 10 environments. Though rarely the focus of attention, the Cisco Router is a foundational element, reliably directing the flow of information and quietly underpinning the operations of the organizations it serves.

Noha Zaki is a seasoned Network Engineer, a meticulous architect of digital infrastructure with a proven track record spanning diverse environments – from the historic halls of The Scindia School to the bustling railway networks of India. Her expertise centers on the seamless integration of routers, switches, and servers, a skillset honed over years of hands-on experience. 

Initially, at The Scindia School (2017-2019), she established and maintained the foundational network, expertly wielding group policies and ensuring robust data protection through diligent backup schedules. She wasn’t simply maintaining a network; she was safeguarding a legacy. This period saw her mastering Cisco router configurations, alongside the administration of both Windows and Linux server environments.

Moving to Asha MMPC in Rajasthan (2019-2020), Noha’s responsibilities broadened to encompass specialized server configurations – DPMCU, Milknet, EMS, and QMS – vital to the organization’s operations. Here, she demonstrated an ability to adapt her skills to industry-specific needs, ensuring the smooth functioning of critical systems.

Currently, as a Sr. Network Engineer at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd. (2021-present), Noha leads the network configuration for Railwire/Railtel, a project of national significance. She’s not just configuring switches; she’s building connectivity for a nation on the move, overseeing everything from the physical infrastructure – earthing and trenching – to the creation of reliable Wi-Fi environments in busy railway stations. Her work directly impacts the passenger experience and the efficiency of India’s rail network. 

Throughout her career, Noha consistently demonstrates a holistic approach, understanding that switches aren’t isolated components but integral parts of a larger, interconnected system – a system she expertly configures *with* and *for* the servers they support. She is a pragmatic problem-solver, equally comfortable with high-level strategy and the granular details of network implementation.

Noha Zaki is a seasoned Network Engineer, a quiet architect of connectivity who ensures information flows seamlessly. Her expertise centers on the intricate world of routers, switches, and servers – the backbone of modern networks. From 2017 to 2019, she honed her skills at The Scindia School, meticulously configuring these systems and establishing robust backup protocols, breathing new life into Cisco router setups. She doesn’t just *install* networks; she maintains them, anticipating needs and ensuring uninterrupted service. 

Moving to Asha MMPC in 2019, Noha’s responsibilities broadened. She became a central figure in managing the entire IT infrastructure, expertly configuring specialized servers – DPMCU, Milknet, EMS, and QMS – alongside her core router and switch duties. This period demonstrated her ability to adapt to diverse environments and understand the specific demands of different systems.

Currently, as a Sr. Network Engineer at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd. since 2021, Noha tackles large-scale projects for Railwire/Railtel, bringing connectivity to vital transportation hubs. She doesn’t shy away from the physical aspects of network deployment, overseeing earthing and trenching alongside the configuration of routers and switches. Her work extends to crafting reliable Wi-Fi environments for bustling railway stations, a testament to her ability to deliver critical infrastructure in demanding public spaces. 

Throughout her career, Noha consistently demonstrates a holistic approach, understanding that routers aren’t isolated components but integral parts of a larger ecosystem, intrinsically linked to the servers they serve. She’s a pragmatic problem-solver, a meticulous planner, and a dedicated professional who builds the invisible infrastructure that keeps the world connected.

The Fortinet Firewall stands as a stalwart guardian of network perimeters, a digital sentry honed by a deep understanding of network traffic and security protocols. It isn’t merely a device, but a core competency, a skill set mastered alongside the intricate architectures of Cisco’s routing and switching world. Where Cisco builds the roads, Fortinet secures the cities. 

This firewall isn’t a solitary tower; it thrives within complex network ecosystems. It speaks the languages of InterVLAN Routing, OSPF, and EIGRP, understanding the flow of data across segmented networks. It’s fluent in the granular control of Access Control Lists (ACLs) and the dynamic adaptability of protocols like STP and HSRP. IPv6 isn’t a future concern, but a present reality it handles with ease. 

Its presence is often found alongside the workhorses of enterprise infrastructure – Cisco routers from the 1800 to 7200 series, and Catalyst switches. It complements the power of HP, Dell, and Lenovo servers, protecting the data they house. While comfortable in a world of structured cabling and modem connections, its true strength lies in its ability to analyze and filter the digital currents flowing through modern networks. 

More than just blocking unwanted access, the Fortinet Firewall represents a proactive defense, a learned expertise in identifying and mitigating threats before they can compromise the network’s integrity. It’s a critical component, a silent protector, and a testament to a comprehensive understanding of networking technologies.

DHCP is a foundational element of this network engineer’s skillset, a quiet but crucial power underpinning the smooth operation of numerous systems. Not a flashy component, but one consistently relied upon – evidenced by years of direct administration across a spectrum of Windows Server iterations, from the legacy 2000 and 2003 platforms through to the more modern 2008 R2 and 2012 R2. 

This isn’t merely configuration; it’s a demonstrated history of ownership. From the academic environment of The Scindia School to the pharmaceutical infrastructure of Teva API India, DHCP has been a consistent responsibility, woven into the broader tapestry of server management. It exists not in isolation, but as a key player within a larger suite of server technologies – Active Directory, DNS, FTP, and more – all working in concert. 

DHCP represents a core competency, a reliable tool honed through practical application and ongoing maintenance, ensuring networks remain accessible and efficiently allocated. It’s a testament to a pragmatic approach, focused on the essential services that keep things running, a silent architect of network connectivity.

A quiet cornerstone of network infrastructure, FTP is a capability expertly wielded by this individual – a digital gatekeeper ensuring seamless file transfer. Not a flashy element, but a vital one, consistently maintained and refined across diverse environments. From the academic halls of The Scindia School to the pharmaceutical precision of Teva API India, FTP has been a trusted tool in their hands. 

They aren’t simply users of FTP; they are its custodians. Capable of configuring and administering it across a spectrum of Windows Server iterations – from the seasoned 2000 and 2003 platforms, through the robust 2008 R2 and 2012 R2 – they demonstrate a deep understanding of its implementation and ongoing management. FTP isn’t a standalone skill, but woven into a broader mastery of server technologies, existing alongside Active Directory, DHCP, and DNS, all working in concert to build and maintain reliable network ecosystems. It’s a foundational element of their expertise, a silent workhorse ensuring data flows smoothly and securely.

Servers are the quiet workhorses underpinning critical operations, consistently managed and configured by skilled engineers like Noha Zaki. They aren’t glamorous, but their reliability is paramount. From 2013-2014, they formed the backbone of Nava Bharat Press (P) Ltd., entrusted to a System Engineer for upkeep, backup, and the smooth functioning of helpdesk support. Later, at Asha MMPC in Rajasthan (2019-2020), servers became more specialized – DPMCU, Milknet, EMS, and QMS Servers each playing a vital role in the dairy’s operations, all under Noha Zaki’s careful configuration and protected by diligently scheduled backups. 

More recently, at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd. (2021-present), these machines have taken on a national scale, forming a crucial component of Railwire/Railtel’s infrastructure across Mumbai and its railway stations. Noha Zaki leads their configuration alongside routers and switches, ensuring connectivity and stability, even extending to the physical groundwork of earthing and trenching to support their operation. They are the silent partners in connectivity, data storage, and process automation, demanding constant attention to detail and a proactive approach to maintenance – a responsibility consistently demonstrated by those who manage them.

Windows Server 2012 R2 appears as a reliable, if somewhat seasoned, workhorse in network infrastructure. Frequently found alongside its siblings, 2008 R2 and 2016, it’s a core component of environments ranging from educational institutions like The Scindia School to pharmaceutical facilities such as Teva API India. It’s not a flashy system, but a foundational one – consistently tasked with critical services. 

This server isn’t often deployed in isolation; it’s a key player in a broader network ecosystem, reliably handling essential functions like Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, FTP, and TFTP. It’s a system that demands consistent upkeep, evidenced by regular health checks, configuration upgrades (often alongside Cisco networking equipment), and diligent backup schedules. 

While capable of standing alone, it frequently operates in a mixed OS environment, comfortably coexisting with Linux servers. It’s a system that’s been actively maintained and administered, suggesting a need for ongoing support and a proven track record of stability within diverse network landscapes. It’s a mature platform, demonstrating longevity and continued relevance in supporting core network services.

Group Policies are the invisible hand shaping the digital landscape of The Scindia School. From January 2017 to May 2019, they were meticulously crafted and maintained, a core skillset of a dedicated administrator who wielded them to configure the school’s network backbone – routers, switches, and servers – with precision. More than just settings, these policies were the architects of consistency, ensuring reliable backups and smooth operation across the entire infrastructure. They weren’t merely *used* with servers; they *defined* how those servers behaved, working in concert with technologies like Active Directory, DHCP, and DNS to create a unified, manageable system. A master of Server Technologies, this administrator understood Group Policies not as isolated tools, but as integral components, seamlessly woven into the fabric of the school’s IT environment, constantly refined and upgraded – particularly the Cisco router configurations – to maintain peak performance and security. They represent a foundational layer of control, a silent guardian of the network’s integrity.
